supply_chain.dependencies: We depend on data centers and other infrastructure operated by third parties, including Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Google Cloud. We rely upon third-party partners to operate certain aspects of our services.
supply_chain.risks: Disruption of or interference at third-party data centers may impact our operations. We rely on third-party hosted infrastructure partners for computing infrastructure. Any disruption at these facilities could adversely impact our business. Supply chain attacks have increased in frequency and severity.

regulatory_risks: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) compliance and potential penalties up to 4% of worldwide revenue
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA) compliance
EU AI Act compliance requirements with various deadlines in coming years
Data localization requirements in countries such as Russia, China, and India
FedRAMP authorization requirements for U.S. federal government contracts
OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax rules (15% minimum tax for large multinational corporations)
Privacy and data protection laws evolving globally
Export restrictions, duties, quotas, tariffs, trade disputes, and sanctions
Government procurement regulations for public sector contracts
Cybersecurity and data breach notification requirements
Employment and immigration laws across multiple jurisdictions
Environmental, social, and governance (ESG) disclosure requirements
Anti-ESG policies and legislation in certain jurisdictions

sanction_risks: Restrictive governmental actions focused on cross-border trade including sanctions
Retaliatory cyber-attacks stemming from the Russia-Ukraine conflict
Trade restrictions and barriers that may prevent offering services to particular markets
